name: GT-jb-spectral
source: HOS, refs/hos/joa-m.md:2431-2433 (3.2.3 + 3.2.4 The spectral theorem), VERBATIM: "If A is a unital Jordan Banach algebra and  $a \in A$  we denote by C(a) the smallest norm-closed Jordan subalgebra of A containing a and 1. Then C(a) is associative. We define the spectrum of a, denoted by Sp a, to be the set of  $\lambda \in \mathbb{R}$  such that  $a \lambda 1$  does not have an inverse in C(a)." and "The spectral theorem. Let A be a unital Jordan Banach algebra. Let  $a \in A$  and suppose C(a) is a JB algebra in an equivalent norm  $\| \| \|$ . Then C(a) is isometrically isomorphic to  $C(\operatorname{Sp} a)$  with respect to  $\| \| \|$ ." For A=V=B(H)_sa (unital JB algebra, GT-jc-is-jb) and a=y: C(y) is isometrically isomorphic to C(Sp y) (continuous real functions on the compact set Sp y), via an isomorphism phi (unital, Jordan, isometric) with phi(y)=iota (the identity function t) by 1.3.5/3.2.4.
